question_text: During the winter solstice in the Northern Hemisphere, which hemisphere receives more direct sunlight and has longer days?

options.label: A
options.text: The Northern Hemisphere, because it is tilted towards the Sun

options.label: B
options.text: The Southern Hemisphere, because it is tilted away from the Sun

options.label: C
options.text: The Southern Hemisphere, because it is tilted towards the Sun

options.label: D
options.text: Neither hemisphere receives more direct sunlight at the solstice

correct_answer: The Southern Hemisphere, because it is tilted towards the Sun
explanation: When the Northern Hemisphere experiences winter solstice, it is tilted away from the Sun. At the same time, the Southern Hemisphere is tilted towards the Sun, so it receives more direct sunlight and experiences summer.
